messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: A couple of bugs that could do with some attention, but a good first release. 1) Clicking on a contact brings up a different one, so it's guesswork as to which entry in the list matches the one you want to send a DM to, for example. 2) T-deck trackball is too sensitive when selecting items and so as the trackball is pressed, it moves to a different item much more than it feels it should. 3) Normal encrypted Group Channels can't be added. 4) I can't see how to delete unused channels once added (the limit of 4 doesn't help). 5) Is there a manual that explains adding and managing contacts, how adverts are handled, etc? There's no criticism here, it's a promising first look.
messages.content_html: A couple of bugs that could do with some attention, but a good first release. 1) Clicking on a contact brings up a different one, so it's guesswork as to which entry in the list matches the one you want to send a DM to, for example. 2) T-deck trackball is too sensitive when selecting items and so as the trackball is pressed, it moves to a different item much more than it feels it should. 3) Normal encrypted Group Channels can't be added. 4) I can't see how to delete unused channels once added (the limit of 4 doesn't help). 5) Is there a manual that explains adding and managing contacts, how adverts are handled, etc? There's no criticism here, it's a promising first look.
messages.reactions_html: 5

messages.reply_html:
meano
Just to note: on the MeshOS store page the map is described as “GPS Map, no tile files required” with “worldwide city names”, and it only calls out a special UK view with a live UK outline. So if maps are currently UK-only in practice, it would be great to clarify this limitation in the description/docs (and ideally in the Map screen), otherwise non-UK users will assume they’re misconfigured when they only see the GPS dot.

messages.author: iron._.nick
messages.text: Thinking over and over again over Liam's comment about who owns what project, concerns me about how MC might get split into multiple paths. I think everyone should sit down and discuss how to unite around a single device UI and features. (T-deck to begin with) To be honest Andy's UI looks modern and handy, but also releasing it half baked (making a video advertising it) and putting a paywall at the very start, looks like a startup cash grab. Devs should be supported, as this is time and money spent on their part. But a united path forward should be found!

Really fast and no more lag on tapping, except one major problem: I cannot send any messages or adverts adverts fail to send when I send a message on Public channel, my repeater has no hits at all Setup: T-Deck+ set to US (Rec) location and has same specs as the repeater Repeater - T-Beam running latest FW on standard 910.525m SF7, CR5, BW 62.5 Also have T-Echo, runs great and adverts register and messages are heard/repeated
messages.content_html:
@andykirby Andy, the MESHOS looks great! All icons work (I have the locked version) and seems to work and man is it snappy compared to the Ripple 7.14! Really fast and no more lag on tapping, except one major problem:
  • I cannot send any messages or adverts
  • adverts fail to send
  • when I send a message on Public channel, my repeater has no hits at all Setup:
  • T-Deck+ set to US (Rec) location and has same specs as the repeater
  • Repeater - T-Beam running latest FW on standard 910.525m SF7, CR5, BW 62.5
  • Also have T-Echo, runs great and adverts register and messages are heard/repeated

messages.reply_html:
andykirby
A bit of a test release for y'all to try! Quite a lot of minor changes, mostly minor tweaks,
  • path set should work now
  • delete contacts, repeaters from the details panel
  • in terminal you can bulk delete repeaters, rooms, chat with /clearcontacts repeaters|rooms|chat
  • Radio Profiles updated to match MeshCore App.
  • Map bug that showed wierd UK outline when no GPS was set.
  • GPS timezone: /gps tz +1 -will set UTC +1 however now the time is not auto synced from GPS, just from the Mesh and you can overide the time in - time set.
 (edited)

messages.author: MeshGyver
messages.text: Tried it for the first time today. My first impressions: It looks good. Thanks for the great work, appreaciated. Especially the pixel sharpness is finally good compared to Ripple OS. What I did notice is that the OS sometimes responds a bit slowly — maybe it’s my device, but that’s what I experienced. In addition, the notification sound is too quiet. It’s barely audible, even with the volume set to maximum. There is also no map background (maybe that will be added later?), and there is only space for 4 hashtag channels, no more. Maybe I missed something? No GPS data is visible (only GPS on/off). I purchased the licensed version. I also noticed that message repeats seem to be missed more often compared to a normal Meshcore node with a smartphone — though that might be coincidence. Then maybe bit more color in the chatwindows menu / windows to give some better visibility instead of everything black? All in all, a lot of potential! Thanks! (edited)
messages.content_html: Tried it for the first time today. My first impressions: It looks good. Thanks for the great work, appreaciated. Especially the pixel sharpness is finally good compared to Ripple OS. What I did notice is that the OS sometimes responds a bit slowly — maybe it’s my device, but that’s what I experienced. In addition, the notification sound is too quiet. It’s barely audible, even with the volume set to maximum. There is also no map background (maybe that will be added later?), and there is only space for 4 hashtag channels, no more. Maybe I missed something? No GPS data is visible (only GPS on/off). I purchased the licensed version. I also noticed that message repeats seem to be missed more often compared to a normal Meshcore node with a smartphone — though that might be coincidence. Then maybe bit more color in the chatwindows menu / windows to give some better visibility instead of everything black? All in all, a lot of potential! Thanks! (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: r00t
messages.text: if I had to do this, I would go for having a submenu in settings with all supported layouts. English, Bulgarian, Czech, etc etc. you scroll into this submenu and select which one you want for input. Usually most people use the stock qwery and a native one. I am not aware of people that use more than two(but it may be possible). Next we decide a keyboard shortcut(sym+alt) that circles trough the one selected, so you do not have to go to settings each time to switch layout. Finally you may display next to the clock on the upper right corner of the screen the input (en, bg, cz, etc etc) (edited)

messages.author: phedders
messages.text: MSB is more logical to me - tell me "7" first and I dont know what month or year that is - could be any 7th in eternity. Tell me 2026 and you've already narrowed it down to at most 366 days. Next narrow it more with a month. I dont start reading out pi backwards starting at the 9trillions bit... (edited)
messages.content_html: MSB is more logical to me - tell me "7" first and I dont know what month or year that is - could be any 7th in eternity. Tell me 2026 and you've already narrowed it down to at most 366 days. Next narrow it more with a month. I dont start reading out pi backwards starting at the 9trillions bit... (edited)

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: The letters in the popup are always shown in capital form: + letter popup: + w → ш, щ + x → ч alone → ю However, if I press sym + # twice to show the popup, the popup then displays both uppercase and lowercase letters.
messages.content_html:
The letters in the popup are always shown in capital form:

+ letter popup:

+ w → ш, щ

+ x → ч

alone → ю However, if I press sym + # twice to show the popup, the popup then displays both uppercase and lowercase letters.
messages.reply_html:
andykirby
and you can access Via # + letter popup:

+ w → ш, щ

+ x → ч

# alone → ю

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: @andykirby I think this would be the best approach, similar to pressing sym + # twice. (edited)
messages.content_html: @andykirby I think this would be the best approach, similar to pressing sym + # twice. (edited)

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: @andykirby Two more things I noticed: 1.Bug: Pressing sym + # twice always shows the same popup with Cyrillic symbols, regardless of the selected language (RU, UA, DE, BG, EN, etc.). 2.Feature idea: It might be useful to allow language switching directly from the bottom-right corner of the screen, where the current localization is shown (BG, DE, etc.), so the user can tap there to change the keyboard layout.
messages.content_html: @andykirby Two more things I noticed: 1.Bug: Pressing sym + # twice always shows the same popup with Cyrillic symbols, regardless of the selected language (RU, UA, DE, BG, EN, etc.). 2.Feature idea: It might be useful to allow language switching directly from the bottom-right corner of the screen, where the current localization is shown (BG, DE, etc.), so the user can tap there to change the keyboard layout.

messages.author: MeshPlateau
messages.text: Plus 1; this was my experience as well. I also encountered another bug where everything works fine for a while, then after some number of reboots (no FW switching, mind you), going to the Public channel crashes/restarts MeshOS when there are unread messages in it. Number of unread message indicators on various channels also go wonky and jump around from crash/restart to restart. The only reliable workaround I found was to change the system setting to disable saving messages. Even leaving that enabled and running the clear messages command in the terminal didn't seem to be a fix. Listing messages in the terminal after a crash also showed incorrect results. If I recall correctly, empty messages with IDs all of "0" or something. Sorry for not sharing sooner. Wanted to verify some of those details first and have been busy.
messages.content_html: Plus 1; this was my experience as well. messages.author: MeshPlateau
messages.text: Installed the 1.1.0 firmware yesterday afternoon finally. Some details and observations: I've got the M5 Launcher installed. I was running Ripple firmware, then switched to MeshOS-TDeck-1.1.0.bin via the M5 Launcher. This bin file was downloaded from the meshcore flasher website "App firmware bin" option. interestingly, I didn't have to set it all up again - the settings from last time were still intact! (Is it using the SD card now?) Various little UI bugs seem to have gone away. automatic time set from incoming messages stopped working some sub menus/components go back to the main menu when hitting backspace, but others leap all the way out to the standby screen I've used it since yesterday, rebooted a few times, and haven't been able to trigger the "messages in public channel" crash that I described before. that said, it will still show unread icons after a reboot on channels that have definitely already been read. automatic repeater addition is now working that's all I can remember atm. I'll give 1.1.1 a try later today.
messages.content_html:
Installed the 1.1.0 firmware yesterday afternoon finally. Some details and observations:
  • I've got the M5 Launcher installed. I was running Ripple firmware, then switched to MeshOS-TDeck-1.1.0.bin via the M5 Launcher. This bin file was downloaded from the meshcore flasher website "App firmware bin" option.
  • interestingly, I didn't have to set it all up again - the settings from last time were still intact! (Is it using the SD card now?)
  • Various little UI bugs seem to have gone away.
  • automatic time set from incoming messages stopped working
  • some sub menus/components go back to the main menu when hitting backspace, but others leap all the way out to the standby screen
  • I've used it since yesterday, rebooted a few times, and haven't been able to trigger the "messages in public channel" crash that I described before.
  • that said, it will still show unread icons after a reboot on channels that have definitely already been read.
  • automatic repeater addition is now working
  • that's all I can remember atm. I'll give 1.1.1 a try later today.
messages.reply_html:
MeshPlateau
Oh, I hadn't seen v1.1 until now. I'll give it a try shortly. Wonder which of these three download options I should pick for use from the M5 launcher... (edited)

messages.author: Eric P.
messages.text: For those with MAP TILE problems, I have solved my problems and here's what I did. First, the SD card was too big, 32 GB or less formatted to FAT. Second, tiles are in the /tiles folder at the root and the zoom levels are in that tiles folder. Third, you only need zoom levels 7-13 for showing in the MeshOS map. I was duped because /sd in terminal showed it mounted and the wallpaper file worked fine. However, maps tiles and the sd card firmware updates wouldn't work. Hope this might help some, cheers. @PiecesAndPawns
messages.content_html: For those with MAP TILE problems, I have solved my problems and here's what I did. First, the SD card was too big, 32 GB or less formatted to FAT. Second, tiles are in the /tiles folder at the root and the zoom levels are in that tiles folder. Third, you only need zoom levels 7-13 for showing in the MeshOS map. I was duped because /sd in terminal showed it mounted and the wallpaper file worked fine. However, maps tiles and the sd card firmware updates wouldn't work. Hope this might help some, cheers. @PiecesAndPawns

For those with MAP TILE problems, I have solved my problems and here's what I did. First, the SD card was too big, 32 GB or less formatted to FAT. Second, tiles are in the /tiles folder at the root and the zoom levels are in that tiles folder. Third, you only need zoom levels 7-13 for showing in the MeshOS map. I was duped because /sd in terminal showed it mounted and the wallpaper file worked fine. However, maps tiles and the sd card firmware updates wouldn't work. Hope this might help some, cheers. @PiecesAndPawns

messages.content_html: The numbered folders are the zoom levels

messages.author: Tom F8COD
messages.text: Hi Travis and @Jade too, I had this on the t-deck, you got to be sure to set the correct baud rate. The gps module on the t-deck plus is 38400bps, the one I put on my old t-deck is 9600bps, you this in the terminal with the /gps baud 9600 . One other thing, when I start the t-deck inside and I try to take it ouside after a while, my gps fails to get a fix. I turn the gps off and on and it gets a fix almost immediately.

i guess meshos is still far from production state.

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: I’m also experiencing GPS issues with MeshOs. I installed TactilityOS ( https://tactilityproject.org/#/) to test the GPS since it includes a dedicated GPS diagnostics app in the settings. With TactilityOS, I was able to get a GPS fix with 12–13 satellites in about 10 seconds while inside my house. Outside, I was able to get a cold start fix with around 22 satellites, again in roughly 10 seconds. This strongly suggests to me that the issue is firmware-related rather than hardware-related. The T-Deck uses a u-blox GNSS module that supports multiple satellite constellations, including GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, and QZSS. I suspect that the initialization of the GNSS module in the affected firmware versions may not be properly configured, which could explain the significantly longer time required to obtain a fix.
messages.content_html: I’m also experiencing GPS issues with MeshOs. I installed TactilityOS (https://tactilityproject.org/#/) to test the GPS since it includes a dedicated GPS diagnostics app in the settings. With TactilityOS, I was able to get a GPS fix with 12–13 satellites in about 10 seconds while inside my house. Outside, I was able to get a cold start fix with around 22 satellites, again in roughly 10 seconds. This strongly suggests to me that the issue is firmware-related rather than hardware-related. The T-Deck uses a u-blox GNSS module that supports multiple satellite constellations, including GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou, and QZSS. I suspect that the initialization of the GNSS module in the affected firmware versions may not be properly configured, which could explain the significantly longer time required to obtain a fix.

Tactility is an operating system that targets ESP32 devices

messages.author: gdob/LZ1WOW
messages.text: By the way, I also tried another T-Deck UI firmware, MC Term ( https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore/) , and the GPS performance there is significantly better. It also supports unlimited offline map tile providers that can be configured directly from the SD card. In addition, the T-Deck can automatically download map tiles over Wi-Fi, which is very convenient since it eliminates the need to download and transfer tiles from a PC. It also supports map control using the keyboard and includes many additional features. There are still some bugs, but in my opinion, it’s definitely worth checking out as well.
messages.content_html: By the way, I also tried another T-Deck UI firmware, MC Term (https://github.com/dabeani/meshcore/), and the GPS performance there is significantly better. It also supports unlimited offline map tile providers that can be configured directly from the SD card. In addition, the T-Deck can automatically download map tiles over Wi-Fi, which is very convenient since it eliminates the need to download and transfer tiles from a PC. It also supports map control using the keyboard and includes many additional features. There are still some bugs, but in my opinion, it’s definitely worth checking out as well.

messages.author: meretechnicality
messages.text: hey everyone! i've been really enjoying MeshOS so far on my t-deck plus, but i've got a few questions: 1) i've noticed that every time i power the device on and off, the time resets to some time in 2024 -- is there a way to either get time changes to stick between power cycles, or get it to automatically download the time from GPS lock? (it CAN get gps signal, but doesn't download the time from it) 2) is there anything specific i have to do to get map tiles to show up, other than throwing them onto /tiles on the SD card? 3) on my t-deck plus, i've still been having touchscreen lag issues, even after installing the TouchScreenFix firmware (which mostly fixed the touchscreen issues on the ripple firmware). is there a fix for this yet? (edited)

messages.author: Eric P.
messages.text: The TDeck+ should get time from the GPS signal once it captures position. You can try to go into the Terminal and use the Time command to set epoch offset. It won't keep time while off. Be sure to use a small SD card, 32 or bellow for map tiles. The individual zoom folders must be on the SD card in the /tiles folder.
messages.content_html: The TDeck+ should get time from the GPS signal once it captures position. You can try to go into the Terminal and use the Time command to set epoch offset. It won't keep time while off. Be sure to use a small SD card, 32 or bellow for map tiles. The individual zoom folders must be on the SD card in the /tiles folder.

messages.author: myshoeisonfire
messages.text: i ended up rebooting the P4 many times, and making sure the micro SD card was fat32.. and readable by the files app.. and (for unrelated reasons) ended up flashing it 3 times BUT after poking around at it, it finally started allowing the GPS to work, and saving contacts to the SD card! maybe flash it, check the files app to make sure it can see the SD card, then walk away and give it some privacy for 30 min to finish whatever its doing after initial install lol
messages.content_html: i ended up rebooting the P4 many times, and making sure the micro SD card was fat32.. and readable by the files app.. and (for unrelated reasons) ended up flashing it 3 times BUT after poking around at it, it finally started allowing the GPS to work, and saving contacts to the SD card! maybe flash it, check the files app to make sure it can see the SD card, then walk away and give it some privacy for 30 min to finish whatever its doing after initial install lol

messages.author: steve fox
messages.text: I have been looking at stuff, no need for a separate charge board, the LGS4056H used in the T-Display P4 is 1A capable, we just need to locate the programing resistor and change it from a 2k (500ma) to a 1k for 1A charging.
messages.content_html: I have been looking at stuff, no need for a separate charge board, the LGS4056H used in the T-Display P4 is 1A capable, we just need to locate the programing resistor and change it from a 2k (500ma) to a 1k for 1A charging.

messages.author: Eric P.
messages.text: For those with MAP TILE problems, I have solved my problems and here's what I did. First, the SD card was too big, 32 GB or less formatted to FAT. Second, tiles are in the /tiles folder at the root and the zoom levels are in that tiles folder. /tiles/16 Third, you only need zoom levels 7-13 for showing in the MeshOS map. I was duped because /sd in terminal showed it mounted and the wallpaper file worked fine. However, maps tiles and the sd card firmware updates wouldn't work. For map files, I used Cyclenerd's offline maps downloader from GitHub. https://github.com/Cyclenerd/offline-map-tile-downloader (edited)
messages.content_html: For those with MAP TILE problems, I have solved my problems and here's what I did. First, the SD card was too big, 32 GB or less formatted to FAT. Second, tiles are in the /tiles folder at the root and the zoom levels are in that tiles folder. /tiles/16 Third, you only need zoom levels 7-13 for showing in the MeshOS map. I was duped because /sd in terminal showed it mounted and the wallpaper file worked fine. However, maps tiles and the sd card firmware updates wouldn't work. For map files, I used Cyclenerd's offline maps downloader from GitHub. https://github.com/Cyclenerd/offline-map-tile-downloader (edited)

messages.author: MeshGyver
messages.text: II have been trying MC Term for a week now, and there is an option to synchronize the time with NTP over Wi-Fi. That works pretty well. MC is mainly considered a LoRa solution from an off-grid perspective (situations without Wi-Fi). However, it would be useful not to depend solely on GPS for time synchronization, especially when sending messages with the correct time and date is crucial. After all, the ESP32 has this capability.
messages.content_html: II have been trying MC Term for a week now, and there is an option to synchronize the time with NTP over Wi-Fi. That works pretty well. MC is mainly considered a LoRa solution from an off-grid perspective (situations without Wi-Fi). However, it would be useful not to depend solely on GPS for time synchronization, especially when sending messages with the correct time and date is crucial. After all, the ESP32 has this capability.

messages.author: Bob LB5JJ - Radiohyperactivity
messages.text: Been trying to search the discord, but didn't fine anything. So I'll ask instead. When moving from using a normal BT companion and the companion app to using MeshOS on the T-Desk - is there a way of bringing over my keys? So that I'll still be the same "person" to whoever I already have conversations going with? If not, is this something that is planned? If you're looking to use MeshCore for private and confidential communication, having a new device show up on the mesh claiming to be someone you know would trigger a (relatively) time consuming task in verifying that this is in fact the same person on a new device... And in some cases, this may not even be possible! Also, being able to import your keys into a new device if your old device was stolen would be very useful to inform your contact that you are no longer in control og a device containing your keys...
messages.content_html: Been trying to search the discord, but didn't fine anything. So I'll ask instead. When moving from using a normal BT companion and the companion app to using MeshOS on the T-Desk - is there a way of bringing over my keys? So that I'll still be the same "person" to whoever I already have conversations going with? If not, is this something that is planned? If you're looking to use MeshCore for private and confidential communication, having a new device show up on the mesh claiming to be someone you know would trigger a (relatively) time consuming task in verifying that this is in fact the same person on a new device... And in some cases, this may not even be possible! Also, being able to import your keys into a new device if your old device was stolen would be very useful to inform your contact that you are no longer in control og a device containing your keys...
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Bob LB5JJ - Radiohyperactivity
messages.text: Anyone have any input on this? Without this feature, the T-Desk is only usable with the standard companion firmware for my use-case. Which is sort of a waste...
messages.content_html: Anyone have any input on this? Without this feature, the T-Desk is only usable with the standard companion firmware for my use-case. Which is sort of a waste...
messages.reply_html:
Bob LB5JJ - Radiohyperactivity
Been trying to search the discord, but didn't fine anything. So I'll ask instead. When moving from using a normal BT companion and the companion app to using MeshOS on the T-Desk - is there a way of bringing over my keys? So that I'll still be the same "person" to whoever I already have conversations going with? If not, is this something that is planned? If you're looking to use MeshCore for private and confidential communication, having a new device show up on the mesh claiming to be someone you know would trigger a (relatively) time consuming task in verifying that this is in fact the same person on a new device... And in some cases, this may not even be possible! Also, being able to import your keys into a new device if your old device was stolen would be very useful to inform your contact that you are no longer in control og a device containing your keys...

messages.author: Eric P.
messages.text: The Repeater option in MeshOS does not operate as a dedicated repeater does as it lacks some repeater functions. It's not recommended for continuous use as a repeater. messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Channel messages separates the sender from the message with a colon. They are in the payload as <sender>: <message> . It isn’t about perfect data, it’s about not allowing the delimiter. (edited)
messages.content_html: Channel messages separates the sender from the message with a colon. They are in the payload as <sender>: <message>. It isn’t about perfect data, it’s about not allowing the delimiter. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
taulen
Sounds like the apps should improve their very rudimentary parsing instead of always relying on receiving perfect data.

messages.author: taulen
messages.text: B. Parsing a Raw Text Stream (Simple Secure Chat/CLI) If you are using a basic serial monitor rather than the formal protocol, MeshCore often outputs: [SenderShortName]: Message content Note: Do not rely on splitting by a colon (:), as users can type colons in their messages. Instead, use Last-Index logic or regex if you are forced to parse text: ^[([^]]+)]:\s(.*)$ This captures the short name inside brackets and everything following the colon as the message.
messages.content_html: B. Instead, use Last-Index logic or regex if you are forced to parse text: ^[([^]]+)]:\s(.*)$ This captures the short name inside brackets and everything following the colon as the message.

messages.author: Scott
messages.text: Prob just a manufacturing/component constraint. The engineering that goes into all the super-slim modern handsets can only really be done by the big players. All those tny ribbon connectors, etc. If you have a heap of readily available IC components, you can only really put them together in relatively chunky ways.
messages.content_html: Prob just a manufacturing/component constraint. The engineering that goes into all the super-slim modern handsets can only really be done by the big players. All those tny ribbon connectors, etc. If you have a heap of readily available IC components, you can only really put them together in relatively chunky ways.

messages.text: HELP Please; I have a Lilygo T-Deck Plus, 433 MHz. My problem is that when I flash the OS version, I cannot choose the frequency, bandwidth, etc. myself — everything is pre-configured. messages.author: uberg33k
messages.text: This is a noob question, so I apologize in advance if this seems annoying. I just got a T-Deck the other day and flashed it with MeshOS. My area doesn't have a lot of repeaters or nodes according to https://meshcore.co.uk/map.html but there are a few. I'm picking up absolutely nothing no matter what I do. I drove to where there's supposedly a repeater, but still nothing. I tried flashing to Ripple to see if it was maybe a bug in MeshOS and that just seemed to brick the device, so flashed back to MeshOS. My question is how can I tell the difference between being in an area with no MC coverage and if there's something wrong with my T-Deck in this instance?

messages.author: davidjay
messages.text: Also take a look at https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/map This map is connected to observer mesh devices that watch for packets on the network. Not being in the map doesn't necessarily mean there's no repeaters near you though. It'll just mean no one has connected an observer. But if there are devices near you on the map, you'll be able to confirm they have been seen recently.
messages.content_html: Also take a look at https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/map This map is connected to observer mesh devices that watch for packets on the network. Not being in the map doesn't necessarily mean there's no repeaters near you though. It'll just mean no one has connected an observer. But if there are devices near you on the map, you'll be able to confirm they have been seen recently.
embeds_html:
MeshCore Analyzer - LetsMesh.net
Real-time MeshCore mesh network analytics, node tracking, packet analysis, and coverage maps.

messages.author: Amaru
messages.text: FYI - if your repeater time is off a bit upon setup, make sure you change the date time in meshOS settings to reflect your current present time to then login as admin on the repeater tab for your repeater and press clock and it’ll update the time/day on the repeater to what your tdeck is tuned to at that moment However what I’ve found is once you do this and then reboot your tdeck (as we know your tdeck will reset its time) - you will need to set the time and date again on your tdeck before trying to login as admin for your repeater on the device should you need to . Otherwise it’ll just keep saying wrong password or something. Just tested this a few times (edited)
messages.content_html: FYI - if your repeater time is off a bit upon setup, make sure you change the date time in meshOS settings to reflect your current present time to then login as admin on the repeater tab for your repeater and press clock and it’ll update the time/day on the repeater to what your tdeck is tuned to at that moment However what I’ve found is once you do this and then reboot your tdeck (as we know your tdeck will reset its time) - you will need to set the time and date again on your tdeck before trying to login as admin for your repeater on the device should you need to . Otherwise it’ll just keep saying wrong password or something. Just tested this a few times (edited)

messages.author: meano
messages.text: Hi @andykirby I’m using a LilyGO T-Display P4 (AMOLED) with MeshOS v1.0 and I’ve already purchased/applied the unlock. I’m having trouble with GPS + Map: GPS seems not to get a fix (no sats / no position shown). The Map icon/menu is present, but the map view stays blank. Because the P4 should have an onboard L76K GPS module, I expected GPS + map to work out of the box. Also, I saw that MeshOS for P4 mentions “GPS Map (custom files required)”, so I’m wondering if my issue is either: 1) GPS not being initialized correctly in v1.0 (or a known bug), and/or 2) I’m not placing the offline map tiles in the correct SD folder structure for MeshOS on the P4. Could you please confirm: Is GPS fully supported/working on T-Display P4 in MeshOS v1.0? What is the exact SD card folder structure expected for map tiles on the P4 (e.g., /tiles/... vs /maps/...)? Any specific format requirements (8-bit PNG, zoom levels, etc.)? Is there a newer P4 build (v1.0.1+ or later) that fixes GPS/map issues? Thanks a lot! Mario
messages.content_html:
Hi @andykirby I’m using a LilyGO T-Display P4 (AMOLED) with MeshOS v1.0 and I’ve already purchased/applied the unlock. I’m having trouble with GPS + Map:
  • GPS seems not to get a fix (no sats / no position shown).
  • The Map icon/menu is present, but the map view stays blank.
Because the P4 should have an onboard L76K GPS module, I expected GPS + map to work out of the box. Also, I saw that MeshOS for P4 mentions “GPS Map (custom files required)”, so I’m wondering if my issue is either: 1) GPS not being initialized correctly in v1.0 (or a known bug), and/or 2) I’m not placing the offline map tiles in the correct SD folder structure for MeshOS on the P4. Could you please confirm:
  • Is GPS fully supported/working on T-Display P4 in MeshOS v1.0?
  • What is the exact SD card folder structure expected for map tiles on the P4 (e.g., /tiles/... vs /maps/...)? Any specific format requirements (8-bit PNG, zoom levels, etc.)?
  • Is there a newer P4 build (v1.0.1+ or later) that fixes GPS/map issues? Thanks a lot!
Mario

messages.author: N'djami
messages.text: Is it compatible with LR1121 ?
messages.content_html: Is it compatible with LR1121 ?
messages.reply_html:
andykirby
======== MeshOS for LilyGo T-Lora Pager ======== Initial release. Install using https://flasher.meshcore.co.uk Select Custom Firmware from the bottom of the list Select the firmware file Confirm the message about erasing the device (as it is a merged FW) Hit Flash and select your device. T-Pager sometimes needs to be put into firmware upload mode, hold the middle button on the front of the device and tap the button on the left (reset) this will force it into firmware upload mode. Usage: Everything is prett self explanatory, the rotary encoder is used to select menu items, rotary press acts as an OK/Confirm Back button <- backs out of menu items. Function button + Rotary: cycles channel tabs, DM and menu In map screen Rotary pans map up and down Rotary+ALT pans left and right, click to zoom. Let me know how it goes!

messages.author: NL11673 / PA9900 / 19RF761
messages.text: How to buy a MeshOS key for the T-Pager?
messages.content_html: How to buy a MeshOS key for the T-Pager?
messages.reply_html:
andykirby
======== MeshOS for LilyGo T-Lora Pager ======== Initial release. Install using https://flasher.meshcore.co.uk Select Custom Firmware from the bottom of the list Select the firmware file Confirm the message about erasing the device (as it is a merged FW) Hit Flash and select your device. T-Pager sometimes needs to be put into firmware upload mode, hold the middle button on the front of the device and tap the button on the left (reset) this will force it into firmware upload mode. Usage: Everything is prett self explanatory, the rotary encoder is used to select menu items, rotary press acts as an OK/Confirm Back button <- backs out of menu items. Function button + Rotary: cycles channel tabs, DM and menu In map screen Rotary pans map up and down Rotary+ALT pans left and right, click to zoom. Let me know how it goes!

messages.author: -= Leon P =-
messages.text: You can import adverts (heard) by long clicking om them. You can import favourite nodes by sharing locally (eg by another ripple t-deck or via the phone- app or by giving a companion advert). First you have to allow: <auto add contacts/ repeaters>!! Otherwise you'll loose them after reset. After you're finished turn that off, otherwise your t-deck will keep filling itself. By long clicking on saved contacts/ repeaters you can manage them. Room servers: same story. (edited)

messages.author: Chris K7RGA
messages.text: Thank you for explaining. I've got her T-Deck on Ripple now but when it was on MeshOS I was hoping I could share the dozen or so contacts of our mutual friends that I have saved on my Tag. Normally with the Meshcore app that's very easy with QR codes. I was hoping I could maybe copy their link to clipboard and DM to her T-Deck where she could copy and add them via clipboard or something, but no dice. I don't want to spend all day trying to wrangle our friends to coordinate an advert right after I enable auto-add contacts. She already had auto-add turned on anyway so there were hundreds of contacts from random people that would have taken forever to remove. I was told MeshOS can't work with room servers, which is important for us as a group, but maybe I was told wrong? A big one is notifications since we hope to use the mesh as a backup to how we activate emergency operators via SMS. We want notifications from our group room and private channel and DMs but not from #public because #public here is going off every 5sec, making notifications useless. Notifications are also a problem with other bluetooth nodes and the Meshcore app since they frequently disconnect from bluetooth, stopping notifications and making a good use case for standalone devices like the T-Deck if the firmware allowed notification management. She was hoping this T-Deck would be more user friendly since she was struggling with the Meshcore iOS app and thought the T-Deck looked more user friendly with a screen and keyboard but I think she's best off sticking with her Tag and Meshcore app. (edited)
messages.content_html: Thank you for explaining. I've got her T-Deck on Ripple now but when it was on MeshOS I was hoping I could share the dozen or so contacts of our mutual friends that I have saved on my Tag. Normally with the Meshcore app that's very easy with QR codes. I was hoping I could maybe copy their link to clipboard and DM to her T-Deck where she could copy and add them via clipboard or something, but no dice. I don't want to spend all day trying to wrangle our friends to coordinate an advert right after I enable auto-add contacts. Notifications are also a problem with other bluetooth nodes and the Meshcore app since they frequently disconnect from bluetooth, stopping notifications and making a good use case for standalone devices like the T-Deck if the firmware allowed notification management. She was hoping this T-Deck would be more user friendly since she was struggling with the Meshcore iOS app and thought the T-Deck looked more user friendly with a screen and keyboard but I think she's best off sticking with her Tag and Meshcore app. (edited)

messages.text: Thank you Andy. So I copy to my iPhone clipboard via Meshcore app, then type /import on my T-Deck? I think I misunderstood how the clipboard transferred devices unless the Meshcore app maybe created a special key in that process that I manually type out in the T-Deck? I tried sharing contacts via Zero Hop advert in the Meshcore app. The option is there but when I try it says it can't because I haven't heard from that node in a while. Even if it's one of my nodes that just adverted. Any idea what's wrong there?
messages.content_html: Thank you Andy. So I copy to my iPhone clipboard via Meshcore app, then type /import on my T-Deck? I think I misunderstood how the clipboard transferred devices unless the Meshcore app maybe created a special key in that process that I manually type out in the T-Deck? messages.reply_html:
andykirby
1. Use another Meshcore node with the contact in it 2. Goto contacts and find the contact 3. tap on the 3 vertical dots to open the context menu and tap share 3. tap on copy link to clipboard 4. on the tdeck open the terminal 5. type /import [then the key you copied] OR easier way just share from another node using Zero Hop advert, just make sure ADD ALL contacts is turned on in the MeshOS settings.

messages.author: Flesh Kimmeh
messages.text: SD cards in general draw almost no power unless you're writing to them, and even then it draws very little. Should be less than 1 mA when idling, a tenth of that while sleeping, and 100 mA or less while writing, with the occasional 200 mA spike. While reading they're probably somewhere in the 50 mA range. Remember reading and writing are likely fairly short bursts, so I'm all it's probably negligible.
messages.content_html: SD cards in general draw almost no power unless you're writing to them, and even then it draws very little. Should be less than 1 mA when idling, a tenth of that while sleeping, and 100 mA or less while writing, with the occasional 200 mA spike. While reading they're probably somewhere in the 50 mA range. Remember reading and writing are likely fairly short bursts, so I'm all it's probably negligible.

messages.author: Eco
messages.text: Great work on multiple language support, I totally missed that. Could you also consider adding Lithuanian support? Needed characters would be: Ą/ą, Č/č, Ę/ę, Ė/ė, Į/į, Š/š, Ų/ų, Ū/ū, Ž/ž Some of the characters overlap with other language from Czech and Polish. Unique characters: Ė/ė, Į/į, Ų/ų, Ū/ū
messages.content_html: Great work on multiple language support, I totally missed that. Could you also consider adding Lithuanian support? Needed characters would be: Ą/ą, Č/č, Ę/ę, Ė/ė, Į/į, Š/š, Ų/ų, Ū/ū, Ž/ž Some of the characters overlap with other language from Czech and Polish. Unique characters: Ė/ė, Į/į, Ų/ų, Ū/ū
messages.reactions_html:
1
1
messages.reply_html:
andykirby
Give this a whirl! Some of the features: 1. DM tab is now generic, tap on it and you'll see a contact list with the most recent conversations at the top. Tap on contact from contact list now opens DM conversation (or allows you to start one) with that contact. Long Press on contact opens contact details. 2. DM notifications! 3. Emoji weirdness, some emoji's were displaying as ?? or a default so now we map emoji's from senders to our limited emoji set and if they don't exist they show as a tofu [] box. Might be annoying at first but better than it was previously and allows me to update the compatible list of emoji's as we go without refactoring. 4. 179 extra glyphs added to the main font: Russian - Full alphabet А-Яа-я + Ё/ё (66 characters) Ukrainian - Additional І/і, Ї/ї, Є/є, Ґ/ґ (8 characters) Bulgarian - Uses Russian Cyrillic characters Latin Extended (Western European): German - Ä/ä, Ö/ö, Ü/ü, ß French - À/à, Â/â, Ç/ç, È/è, É/é, Ê/ê, Ë/ë, Î/î, Ï/ï, Ô/ô, Ù/ù, Û/û Spanish - Ñ/ñ Nordic (Swedish, Norwegian, Danish) - Å/å, Æ/æ, Ø/ø Central/Eastern European: Polish - Ą/ą, Ć/ć, Ę/ę, Ł/ł, Ń/ń, Ó/ó, Ś/ś, Ź/ź, Ż/ż Czech - Č/č, Ď/ď, Ě/ě, Ň/ň, Ř/ř, Š/š, Ť/ť, Ů/ů, Ž/ž Slovak - Same as Czech + Á/á, Í/í, Ľ/ľ, Ĺ/ĺ, Ŕ/ŕ, Ú/ú, Ý/ý Romanian - Ă/ă, Ș/ș, Ț/ț Turkish - Ğ/ğ, İ/ı, Ş/ş 5. Support for 9 international keyboards for inputting text: Layout Code Language Special Characters EN LAYOUT_QWERTY English Standard QWERTY (no remapping) DE LAYOUT_GERMAN German ä ö ü ß (remaps a→ä, o→ö, u→ü, s→ß) FR LAYOUT_FRENCH French é è ê ë ç ES LAYOUT_SPANISH Spanish ñ á é í ó ú PL LAYOUT_POLISH Polish ą ć ę ł ń ó ś ź ż CZ LAYOUT_CZECH Czech/Slovak á č ď é ě í ň ó ř š ť ú ů ý ž RU LAYOUT_RUSSIAN Russian Full ЙЦУКЕН Cyrillic alphabet UA LAYOUT_UKRAINIAN Ukrainian Full Cyrillic + І Ї Є Ґ BG LAYOUT_BULGARIAN Bulgarian Phonetic Cyrillic layout How to switch layouts: Press Sym and * to cycle through layouts Current layout is shown in the keyboard indicator (e.g., "RU", "DE", etc.) The Latin layouts (DE, FR, ES, PL, CZ) remap specific keys to accented characters, while the Cyrillic layouts (RU, UA, BG) provide full alphabet remapping from the physical QWERTY keys. Press sym+# then quickly hit a key to open an accented character overlay, tap to select. THIS WILL LIKELY NEED TWEAKS SO FEEL FREE TO FEEDBACK ( BUT IT SHOULD GET YOU STARTED :-)) 6. GPS Fixes: sorted the timezone issue, and when GPS acquires a fix your time will be set and your timezone applied. Mesh time sync removed. No need to /gps sync it will do it automatically. 7. Tile support for maps, Ripple /tiles on the SD known to work but I will post my python tile download script shortly. Double tap on screen or press trackball to cycle through zoom levels. 8. Lots of bug fixes, tweaks etc. (edited)

messages.author: jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt)
messages.text: I already submitted a GitHub issue for this https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/2038 and although there is library code available on the Lilygo T-Deck repo for the PCF8563/BM8563 Real Time Clock, it's definitely not enabled for the T-Deck. However, my T-Deck Plus definitely does not set or synchronise date and time using GPS. (edited)
messages.content_html: I already submitted a GitHub issue for this https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/2038 and although there is library code available on the Lilygo T-Deck repo for the PCF8563/BM8563 Real Time Clock, it's definitely not enabled for the T-Deck. However, my T-Deck Plus definitely does not set or synchronise date and time using GPS. (edited)
embeds_html:
[MeshOS] Current date and time not retained during power cycle on T...
Description: After setting the date and time in MeshOS on the T-Deck Plus, I expected this value to be stored and persist during any reboot, power down or firmware upgrade operation. What actually ...
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: As a point of interest, I had the same issue with my T-Deck Plus and it was easily overcome by ensuring I powered on for first boot with the T-Deck in view of the sky. I also believe @andykirby has enabled the time sync from the mesh, providing the nodes in the mesh hold the correct time. ALL the devices without GPS need the clocks set after a powerup and all revert back to May 2024.
messages.content_html: As a point of interest, I had the same issue with my T-Deck Plus and it was easily overcome by ensuring I powered on for first boot with the T-Deck in view of the sky. I also believe @andykirby has enabled the time sync from the mesh, providing the nodes in the mesh hold the correct time. ALL the devices without GPS need the clocks set after a powerup and all revert back to May 2024.
messages.reply_html:
jeg1972 (Dunston/LobleyHill Rpt)
I already submitted a GitHub issue for this https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/2038 and although there is library code available on the Lilygo T-Deck repo for the PCF8563/BM8563 Real Time Clock, it's definitely not enabled for the T-Deck. However, my T-Deck Plus definitely does not set or synchronise date and time using GPS. (edited)

messages.reactions_html:

messages.author: George-GM1DSV
messages.text: I get your point, but consider this scenario. Andy works hard to write MeshOS and makes it free to use with premium features to unlock for a small fee. If the source code is open source (hence the meaning of open source) it would not take long for anyone with a modicum of coding skill to remove the portion of code that controls the feature restrictions. This would then mean no more income for Andy, no more tech support for us and one very betrayed Andy. You get the picture on this? The code for the premium access must always remain closed source to protect the copyright and intellectual property of the writer.

messages.author: SM-
messages.text: Yes, apart from Andy is saying about the MeshOS for Mac, I am using Mac terminal to run commands in my T-Deck Plus. You can use "screen" command on your Mac, but I prefer "tio" command, which is installable via homebrew on your Mac. I connect T-Deck via USB port, and then run "ls /dev/tty.* " command. It will show your device: it's either tty.usbmodem101 or tty.usbmodem1101. Then, I run "tio -b 38400 -f none /dev/tty.usbmodem101" to connect. To quit, I run "Ctrl-T, Q". Maybe there is a better way but this is what I use.
messages.content_html: Yes, apart from Andy is saying about the MeshOS for Mac, I am using Mac terminal to run commands in my T-Deck Plus. You can use "screen" command on your Mac, but I prefer "tio" command, which is installable via homebrew on your Mac. I connect T-Deck via USB port, and then run "ls /dev/tty.* " command. It will show your device: it's either tty.usbmodem101 or tty.usbmodem1101. Then, I run "tio -b 38400 -f none /dev/tty.usbmodem101" to connect. To quit, I run "Ctrl-T, Q". Maybe there is a better way but this is what I use.

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: @andykirby Couple of questions with the MeshOS P4 (TFT/LED edition) v1.0.1: Is there a way to enter text in Terminal so I can set the UTC and check the baud rate? When I open terminal no text entry option comes up. Will there be a way to set two-byte outgoing paths soon? If I set a path to a repeater as 3601, it saves as 01, for example. Also looking forward to when we can save a list of favourite contacts. Will there be capital letters or emojis allowed in the MeshOS P4 usernames soon? I tried changing mine and, first off, typing more than one capital letter - turning shift on then off again caused it to delete letters instead of going back into lower case - and then when I did figure out how to add capitals and sent a test message it didn't apply to the username. And is there a way to disable the vibration? Or change the screen brightness (other than standby mode) so it doesn't chew through the power so fast? messages.author: Amaru
messages.text: a few things i may add, re the built in maps, everywhere else outside the UK, the built in map seems to have markings of all the major cities. ex: i am in Dallas, Texas, USA and all the major cities in the US are marked correctly. if you want map tiles, as the other stated, put them on SD card but make sure when you test it, you zoom in to whatever tile levels you have. so if you only have 9x to 13x like me, the first few zoom levels in the map section won't populate anything until I zoom in enough on the nodes discovered thing, only companions will populate in that section of "contacts" in meshOS and repeaters will be in the "repeater tab". Once you hear adverts from any of them, they sometimes populate as "contacts" IN the MC app via BT but in MeshOS on the device, they will appear seperate and companions and repeaters will not be in the same sections.

messages.text: 125kbps or 500kbps, so 8 symbols per bit or 2 symbols per bit (1M symbols per second)

messages.author: b34
messages.text: IOS Bluetooth mesh network makes airtags and find my phone work using Apple devices. The range can be up to around a 1000 metres between devices. Since on device scanning was made possible it also transmits know your customer data to Apple servers six times a second and cannot be turned off. It continues to transmit even after the battery shows flat does so for a further five months. Google, Amazon and others have their own versions

messages.author: Juggern4ught (SW Brisbane)
messages.text: @Timmeh8604 my exact command after adjusting the baud rate and connecting was: /gps tz +10 once I did that UTC: 10:53 - 15/5/2024 Local: 20:53 - 15/5/2024 (UTC+10) It now remains persistant on restart and actually syncs up as soon as it is outside and gets a GPS sync I used Putty and a Windows device FYI (edited)
messages.content_html: @Timmeh8604 my exact command after adjusting the baud rate and connecting was: /gps tz +10 once I did that UTC: 10:53 - 15/5/2024 Local: 20:53 - 15/5/2024 (UTC+10) It now remains persistant on restart and actually syncs up as soon as it is outside and gets a GPS sync I used Putty and a Windows device FYI (edited)

messages.author: woketothetruth
messages.text: Questions for any LILYGO T-Display P4 owners/developers out there: I purchased four of them, and recently MeshOS licensed two (so far): 1.) From a hardware standpoint, is anything other the LoRa radio enabled? a.) i.e. Bluetooth, or b.) Wi-Fi, or c.) Ethernet 2.) If NOT, are there any plans to do so in the future? 3.) Has anyone written a "User Guide" for MeshOS on the LILYGO T-Display P4 ? 4.) If NOT, how do you - a.) Exit from "Standby Mode" once activated? b.) Do the two buttons on the left side of the device actually do anything? c.) What is the proper procedure for loading "map tiles" onto the micro SD Card? I could be mistaken due to the fact that I am a complete n00b at MeshCore, but I assumed that these devices would be at least as capable as other companion devices that cost half as much. @andykirby seemed so enthusiastic in his YouTube video https://youtu.be/z6IOVYemF20?si=v_fQuXu0aREAQeJC about this device, I imagined that development would have been much further along, some four months later. . .

messages.author: woketothetruth
messages.text: @andykirby So, I performed the test as promised. My results were interesting. . . 1.) Went outside, and turned on the LILYGO T-Display P4 2.) After it booted up, I set it face down on a flat surface, with a clear view of the sky 3.) I left it outside for 15 minutes 4.) Returned to check if GPS was working after 15 minutes 5.) Picked up the P4 and found the display was off . I assumed the battery was dead 6.) Went back inside and put the P4 on the charger. Battery was at 95% 7.) I grabbed my second LILYGO T-Display P4, and repeated steps 1 through 5 8.) I put the second P4 on the charger, and it was at 92% 9.) WTF?!?!?!? Does the LILYGO T-Display P4 have a thermal sensor that automatically turns it off when it reaches a certain temperature? It was 46 degrees Fahrenheit (7.7 degrees Celsius) outside. I have cycled the batteries in these units 3 or 4 times each, and they have NEVER just turned themselves off for no reason. What gives?
messages.content_html: @andykirby So, I performed the test as promised. My results were interesting. . . 1.) Went outside, and turned on the LILYGO T-Display P4 2.) After it booted up, I set it face down on a flat surface, with a clear view of the sky 3.) I left it outside for 15 minutes 4.) Returned to check if GPS was working after 15 minutes 5.) Picked up the P4 and found the display was off . I assumed the battery was dead 6.) Went back inside and put the P4 on the charger. Battery was at 95% 7.) I grabbed my second LILYGO T-Display P4, and repeated steps 1 through 5 8.) I put the second P4 on the charger, and it was at 92% 9.) WTF?!?!?!? Does the LILYGO T-Display P4 have a thermal sensor that automatically turns it off when it reaches a certain temperature? It was 46 degrees Fahrenheit (7.7 degrees Celsius) outside. I have cycled the batteries in these units 3 or 4 times each, and they have NEVER just turned themselves off for no reason. What gives?
messages.reply_html:
woketothetruth
I will test that out, and post feedback here. Thank you!

messages.author: zindello (VK2MES)
messages.text: Hey. Had a look at this today as I got a really cheap T-Deck that required some parts swapping from a donor to get it working. I really like the user experience of MeshOS - but I'm on Ripple right now due to a few things that are really hard to do on MeshOS. Custom radio settings are really hard. A not insignificant portion of AU users are on a setting that's not currently a preset, having to drop to the command line to set that, makes it super difficult. Region Support. This one is going to be critical to my use case. It may be that I missed it in the UI, but I couldn't see anywhere obvious to configure regions. Without this, the OS is a non-starter for me unfortunately. Which is a shame, as I really like the look and feel of the OS, it's just lacking in a few critical places for me to use it unfortunately.
messages.content_html: Hey. Had a look at this today as I got a really cheap T-Deck that required some parts swapping from a donor to get it working. A not insignificant portion of AU users are on a setting that's not currently a preset, having to drop to the command line to set that, makes it super difficult. Region Support. This one is going to be critical to my use case. It may be that I missed it in the UI, but I couldn't see anywhere obvious to configure regions. Without this, the OS is a non-starter for me unfortunately. Which is a shame, as I really like the look and feel of the OS, it's just lacking in a few critical places for me to use it unfortunately.

messages.author: Brandon Parker, N0PAR
messages.text: Many of us have invested significant time and resources into developing our mesh networks..purchasing equipment, building systems, educating others, and deploying within our communities. It’s essential that both the MeshCore team and the others collaborate effectively and move forward together as a unified group. Fragmentation will only undermine these efforts. If we don’t maintain alignment, users may become frustrated, abandon their devices or nodes, and ultimately slow the progress of the entire project due to internal challenges.

Since the algorithm is out now, here's a (vibe coded) keygen in bash: #!/usr/bin/env bash mac=$(tr -d ':- ' <<< "$1" | tr a-f A-F) [[ $mac =~ ^[0-9A-F]{12}$ ]] || { echo "invalid mac" >&2; exit 1; } h=$(printf '%d' "'${mac:0:1}") for ((i=1; i<12; i++)); do c=$(printf '%d' "'${mac:i:1}") h=$(( (h*33 + c) & 0xFFFFFFFF )) done x=(0x4D 0x43 0x50 0x50) for i in 0 1 2 3; do b=$(( ((h >> (24 - i*8)) & 0xFF) ^ x[i] )) printf '%02X' "$b" done printf '\n'
